# Test-Driven Development with Python, Third Edition (Harry J.W. Percival)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Getting Django Set Up Using a Functional Test](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/01-getting-django-set-up-using-a-functional-test.md): already_exists
    - [Extending Our Functional Test Using the unittest Module](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/02-extending-our-functional-test-using-the-unittest-module.md): already_exists
    - [Testing a Simple Home Page with Unit Tests](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/03-testing-a-simple-home-page-with-unit-tests.md): already_exists
    - [What Are We Doing with All These Tests? (And, Refactoring)](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/04-what-are-we-doing-with-all-these-tests-and-refactoring.md): Test-Driven Development (TDD) workflows in Python (Django and unittest), including functional testing with Selenium WebDriver, unit testing for web views and templates, code refactoring, and debugging test failures or configuring HTML/CSS templates.
    - [Saving User Input: Testing the Database](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/05-saving-user-input-testing-the-database.md): Implementing user input features, handling HTTP POST requests, creating or modifying Django models, writing database migrations, or constructing unit and functional tests related to database persistence and form submissions in Python/Django applications.
    - [Improving Functional Tests: Ensuring Isolation and Removing Magic Sleeps](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/06-improving-functional-tests-ensuring-isolation-and-removing-magic-sleeps.md): already_exists
    - [Working Incrementally](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/07-working-incrementally.md): already_exists
    - [Prettification: Layout and Styling, and What to Test About It](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/08-prettification-layout-and-styling-and-what-to-test-about-it.md): Tasks involving frontend UI styling, layout implementation (e.g., Bootstrap, CSS), handling Django static files, and writing tests (Functional and Unit) for aesthetics, layout, and HTML structure.
    - [Containerization aka Docker](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/09-containerization-aka-docker.md): already_exists
    - [Making Our App Production-Ready](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/10-making-our-app-production-ready.md): already_exists
    - [Getting a Server Ready for Deployment](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/11-getting-a-server-ready-for-deployment.md): already_exists
    - [Infrastructure as Code: Automated Deployments with Ansible](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/12-infrastructure-as-code-automated-deployments-with-ansible.md): already_exists
    - [Splitting Our Tests into Multiple Files, and a Generic Wait Helper](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/13-splitting-our-tests-into-multiple-files-and-a-generic-wait-helper.md): already_exists
    - [Validation at the Database Layer](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/14-validation-at-the-database-layer.md): Implementing data validation in a Django application, specifically working with Django models, views handling POST requests, form rendering in templates, URL routing refactoring, and writing unit or functional tests for these components.
    - [A Simple Form](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/15-a-simple-form.md): Django forms, input validation, moving logic out of Django views, rendering HTML forms via Django templates, or writing tests for form validation and saving.
    - [More Advanced Forms](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/16-more-advanced-forms.md): Creating, modifying, or refactoring Django forms, implementing database-layer or form-layer validation (specifically uniqueness or duplicate prevention constraints), writing view or model unit tests, managing Django CSS/UI presentation logic within forms, or troubleshooting IntegrityError and ValidationError exceptions.
    - [A Gentle Excursion into JavaScript](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/17-a-gentle-excursion-into-javascript.md): already_exists
    - [Deploying Our New Code](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/18-deploying-our-new-code.md): already_exists
    - [User Authentication, Spiking, and De-Spiking](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/19-user-authentication-spiking-and-de-spiking.md): already_exists
    - [Using Mocks to Test External Dependencies](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/20-using-mocks-to-test-external-dependencies.md): Writing, modifying, or debugging Python unit tests that interact with external dependencies (e.g., email servers, payment gateways, third-party APIs), or implementing/testing authentication backends and branching logic in web applications (specifically Django).
    - [Using Mocks for Test Isolation](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/21-using-mocks-for-test-isolation.md): Writing or refactoring unit tests in Python using the unittest and unittest.mock libraries, particularly within a Django context, with a focus on isolating system components, handling external dependencies, and addressing combinatorial explosion of test cases.
    - [Test Fixtures and a Decorator for Explicit Waits](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/22-test-fixtures-and-a-decorator-for-explicit-waits.md): The domain involves interacting with, creating, or modifying Functional Tests (FTs), specifically when using Selenium Webdriver in a Python/Django environment, focusing on optimizing test speed, skipping repetitive UI workflows (like authentication), setting up test data (fixtures), or refactoring explicit wait/polling mechanisms.
    - [Debugging and Testing Server Issues](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/23-debugging-and-testing-server-issues.md): Debugging and testing Django applications deployed to Docker containers, staging, or production environments, specifically focusing on resolving server errors with DEBUG=False, adapting functional tests for remote/persistent databases, secure secrets management, and automated remote test data setup/teardown.
    - [Finishing “My Lists”: Outside-In TDD](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/24-finishing-my-lists-outside-in-tdd.md): The domain covers the implementation of new features, system architecture design, template refactoring, and test writing (Functional and Unit Tests) specifically within a Django or MVC web framework context using Test-Driven Development (TDD).
    - [CI: Continuous Integration](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/25-ci-continuous-integration.md): Continuous Integration (CI) configuration, pipeline creation, automated testing workflows, debugging remote CI failures, running headless browser tests, or configuring GitHub Actions/GitLab CI for Python (Django) and JavaScript (Jasmine) codebases.
    - [The Token Social Bit, the Page Pattern, and an Exercise for the Reader](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/26-the-token-social-bit-the-page-pattern-and-an-exercise-for-the-reader.md): Functional Testing in Python with Selenium, Test Suite Refactoring, UI Interaction Testing, Multi-User Simulation, and Django Feature Implementation (specifically social/sharing features).
    - [Fast Tests, Slow Tests, and Hot Lava](.roo/vendor/python/test-driven-development-with-python-third-edition-harry-j-w-percival/27-fast-tests-slow-tests-and-hot-lava.md): Writing tests, refactoring test suites, optimizing test execution speed, separating business logic from framework/database dependencies, designing application architecture, or troubleshooting overly complex, slow, or mock-heavy test suites.
